reasoning: The base rate of vaccine development is very low, normally taking many years from initial research to clinical trials and approval. Wikipedia currently shows no vaccine for Colorado tick fever as of July 2025. A review of the literature up to early 2025 indicates that significant effort is still in the purely basic science stage (e.g., reverse genetics systems established in February 2025, in-silico "reverse vaccinology" design published in April 2024). There are no clinical trials underway for a CTF vaccine, nor is there any mention of a candidate entering advanced development. Given the timelines required, it is virtually impossible that a vaccine will be successfully developed and added to the relevant Wikipedia list by August to November 2025, or even February 2026. Therefore, the probability starts very low and only increases very slightly for the later dates to account for the infinitesimally small chance of a rapid off-label approval or error on Wikipedia.
